Hazmat Training Platform

Train Smarter.
Respond Better.

Hazmat Tactical provides hazmat training tools for operations-level firefighters and hazmat technicians. Generate randomized drill scenarios, practice decon decision-making, train container recognition, and sharpen scene reading skills — all aligned to NFPA 470, OSHA HAZWOPER, and ERG 2024.

Start Training → Try First-In Prompt
Quick Access

Jump straight into any module or explore the full toolkit. Aligned to NFPA 470, OSHA HAZWOPER, and ERG 2024.

Hazmat Toolkits

Decision-Support Tools

On-screen tactical references for training evolutions. Built for speed, accuracy, and real-world conditions.

Basic Edition — Tactical Tool

Basic Hazmat Tactical Tool

Your on-screen tactical reference during training. Look up chemicals, assess vapor behavior, and visualize the downwind plume — all in one place.

  • Chemical ID — REL, TWA, STEL, IDLH by name or UN number
  • Vapor density & specific gravity behavior badges
  • ERG isolation distances with spill size & day/night toggle
  • ERG-accurate teardrop plume model with wind direction slider
  • Air monitoring meter alarm reference
  • SCBA entry timer with air supply alerts
  • Note: Alarm limits, percentages, and PPM values are reference benchmarks. Users should verify and convert values to match the specific meters and equipment used by their organization.
Open Basic Tool
Basic Edition — Drill Generator

Basic Drill Generator

Generate complete, structured drill scenarios in seconds. Built for instructors who need ready-to-run tabletop and practical evolutions without hours of prep.

  • 17 chemicals across toxic gas, flammable, corrosive & radiological categories
  • 13 incident environments — highway to hospital to rail yard
  • Operations, Technician, and All-Levels output modes
  • Tactical site diagram with dynamic wind direction
  • ERG isolation data, PPE recommendations & JPR citations
  • Decontamination section included in every drill
Open Drill Generator
Advanced Edition — Tactical Tool

Advanced Hazmat Tactical Tool

Everything in the Basic tool plus a full suite of technical calculators for hazmat technicians who need to go deeper than ERG distances.

  • All Basic Tactical Tool features included
  • LEL → PPM Converter with chemical-specific correction
  • VOC Correction Factor calculator (85+ chemicals)
  • GEBMO behavioral modeling — mechanical, thermal & chemical stress
  • Inverse Square Law calculator for radiation stay-time
  • Reactivity Check & Containment Compatibility reference
  • Note: Alarm limits, percentages, and PPM values are reference benchmarks. Users should verify and convert values to match the specific meters and equipment used by their organization.
Open Advanced Tool
Advanced Edition — Drill Generator

Advanced Drill Generator

Full CBRNE scenario generation with complex multi-layer injects, advanced equipment recommendations, and print-ready output for laminated drill cards.

  • Full CBRNE coverage including radiological & WMD scenarios
  • Multi-level inject system — escalating complexity per drill
  • 40+ instrument equipment library with field directives
  • Incompatibility warning callouts in scenario output
  • 5-participant signature block for documented training records
  • Print-ready PDF output for laminated drill cards
Open Advanced Generator
About Us

Built by a Firefighter.

Hazmat Tactical™ was built by a Chicago firefighter and hazmat technician with a singular mission: to provide highly accessible, fiercely practical training resources for responders.

Whether you're an instructor needing fresh drill ideas, an officer leading tabletop sessions, or a department looking for an affordable solution — especially volunteer squads — this platform is built to help you prepare for the real thing.

Please note: The tools generated on this platform are for training purposes only and must never be utilized to dictate tactics during an active incident.

Disclaimer

All materials provided by Hazmat Tactical, including automated drill scenarios and tactical calculators, are intended for educational and training purposes only. These resources are designed to supplement, not replace, the Standard Operating Guidelines (SOGs) and safety protocols established by your Authority Having Jurisdiction (AHJ).

While every effort is made to ensure mathematical and technical accuracy based on NFPA 470 standards, Hazmat Tactical LLC assumes no liability for the misapplication of this data in live-incident environments. Hazardous materials response is inherently dangerous; always consult your AHJ, local protocols, and onsite Safety Officer before making operational decisions.

If feedback is submitted, any misinformation will be researched and corrected accordingly. Hazmat Tactical LLC is not affiliated with any government agency or company listed or referenced on this site.
